summary: Completed the remaining official-source candidate pass for Evidently, W&B Weave, LlamaIndex evaluators, Haystack evaluation, and OpenAI Evals. Classified framework-native tools and deprecated OpenAI Evals as caveat rows rather than default primary stack choices, and saved a compact evidence note for the next analysis/synthesis step.
next_angle: Move into analysis: consolidate all 15 candidate rows, assign primary vs caveat/exclusion status, define workbook scoring dimensions and weights, and separately verify deployment/governance attributes before scoring them.
open_questions: Which exact candidates should be primary workbook rows versus caveat/exclusion rows after consolidation across all iterations?

What scoring weights should the workbook use for RAG-specific metrics, repeatable CI/regression workflows, observability, datasets, human review, integration fit, deployment/governance, and evidence maturity?
Which deployment/governance attributes can be publicly verified for each primary candidate: OSS license, self-hosting, SaaS availability, RBAC, data retention, and enterprise controls?

sources.title: Evidently documentation: RAG evals
sources.note: Official Evidently tutorial for evaluating RAG retrieval and generation quality with a local open-source workflow, pandas dataframe output, visual reports, and optional platform upload.

sources.title: W&B Weave documentation: Evaluate RAG applications
sources.note: Official Weave guide showing retrieval-step tracing and RAG response evaluation with LLM judges, context precision, datasets, and scorers.

sources.title: LlamaIndex documentation: Evaluating
sources.note: Official LlamaIndex framework docs covering response evaluation, retrieval evaluation, faithfulness, relevancy, semantic similarity, guideline adherence, synthetic question generation, and retrieval metrics.

sources.title: Haystack documentation: Model-Based Evaluation
sources.note: Official Haystack docs for model-based RAG pipeline evaluation, context relevance, faithfulness/groundedness, local evaluator models, and integrations with Ragas and DeepEval.

sources.title: OpenAI API documentation: Working with evals
sources.note: Official OpenAI Evals API docs describing eval creation/runs and stating the Evals platform deprecation timeline: read-only on 2026-10-31 and shutdown on 2026-11-30.

findings.text: Evidently is a plausible primary matrix row for notebook/report-driven RAG quality checks: official docs demonstrate a local open-source workflow that evaluates retrieval context quality and generation quality, then produces dataframe and visual report outputs.

W&B Weave is a plausible primary matrix row for teams that want managed observability plus evaluation: its official RAG tutorial tracks retrieval steps, evaluates responses with LLM judges, and uses datasets/scorers inside Weave evaluations.

LlamaIndex evaluators should be treated as a framework-fit caveat row unless the production RAG stack already uses LlamaIndex. The official docs are strong on response/retrieval evaluation and generated test questions, but the capability is primarily framework-native rather than a standalone evaluation platform.

Haystack evaluation should be treated as a framework-fit caveat row unless the production RAG stack already uses Haystack. Official docs cover component and pipeline evaluation, RAG model-based evaluation, statistical retriever metrics, and Ragas/DeepEval integrations, but the strongest value is inside Haystack pipelines.

OpenAI Evals should not be scored as a long-term primary RAG evaluation stack because official OpenAI docs now mark the Evals platform as deprecated, with read-only status for existing users on 2026-10-31 and shutdown on 2026-11-30. It belongs in a caveat or legacy row unless the final matrix explicitly covers OpenAI-specific evaluation surfaces.

The analysis phase should separate capability scoring from packaging/governance scoring. Public eval capability docs are enough to classify RAG metrics, CI/regression fit, observability, datasets, and framework fit, but license, self-hosting, SaaS, RBAC, retention, and enterprise controls need dedicated public-source verification.

analysis_artifacts.note: Compact evidence table classifying the five remaining candidates and reducing the framework-native/OpenAI Evals open questions for the next analysis phase.

adequacy.goal_alignment: This was a search-phase iteration, not the adequacy gate. It materially improves goal alignment by completing official-source evidence coverage for the remaining named candidates.
adequacy.coverage_summary: All five remaining named candidates now have official-source evidence and initial matrix treatment. The full candidate set still needs consolidation, scoring design, and governance/deployment verification before synthesis.

covered_requirements.requirement: Use current public sources and prefer official documentation, GitHub repositories, release docs, and vendor docs over blog summaries.
covered_requirements.evidence: Used official docs from Evidently, W&B Weave, LlamaIndex, Haystack, and OpenAI.
Classified LlamaIndex and Haystack as framework-fit caveat rows unless the target stack already uses those frameworks.
Classified OpenAI Evals as caveat/legacy because official docs state platform deprecation dates.

coverage_gaps.gap: Deployment/governance attributes still need public-source verification across primary candidates before scoring.
Consolidated workbook schema and scoring weights have not yet been produced.

evidence_risks.risk: Evaluation capability pages do not by themselves prove license, hosting, RBAC, retention, or enterprise controls.

adequacy.recommended_next_angle: Build a consolidated evidence table from iterations 1-3, mark primary/caveat/exclusion status, and design the scoring workbook schema while flagging governance fields that require separate verification.

validation_evidence.reason: Five distinct official documentation sources were used for the remaining candidate set.
No private data, local-only tool names, contact data, or non-public sources were included in the research result.
Search coverage for the named remaining candidates is complete enough to move to analysis, while final adequacy remains not started.
